stories.title: D11 Final Graduation Day — Achieve, Tesla, Bijou and Odyssey Today

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
D11 graduation: Achieve High, Tesla STEM, and Bijou School walk the stage TODAY at 9 AM at Ed Robson Arena.

Odyssey Charter follows at 3 PM. Last day for D11 students: May 22.

d11.org/calendars/graduations

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
D11 Class of 2026 — that's a wrap.

Achieve, Tesla, Bijou this morning. Odyssey this afternoon. All graduating at Ed Robson Arena.

Last day of D11 school: Friday, May 22.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 AM MT

Today wraps up D11's graduation week — and it's a big one.

This morning at 9:00 AM MT, students from Achieve High, Tesla STEM High School, and Bijou School walk the stage at Ed Robson Arena on the Colorado College campus. This afternoon at 3:00 PM MT, Odyssey Elementary Charter School holds its ceremony at the same venue.

It's been quite a week for Colorado Springs families. Palmer graduated Monday, Coronado and Mitchell on Tuesday — and today brings the final ceremonies of the 2025-2026 school year for District 11.

The last day of school for all D11 students is tomorrow, Friday, May 22. If your family is wrapping up a chapter this week, we're celebrating with you.

Livestreams are available at d11.org/calendars/graduations.

Which D11 school had a graduate this week in your family? Tell us in the comments.

---

x_posts.label: Post 2-1

3185 Venetucci Blvd, COS

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Congratulations to the D49 Class of 2026 — Falcon, Vista Ridge, and Sand Creek all cross the stage Saturday at Broadmoor World Arena.

What a week for Colorado Springs families.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 10:00 AM MT

District 49 families — your graduation weekend is almost here.

This Saturday, May 23, three D49 high schools hold commencement ceremonies at Broadmoor World Arena (3185 Venetucci Blvd). Here's the schedule:

Falcon High School: 1:00 PM MT
Vista Ridge High School: 5:00 PM MT
Sand Creek High School: also Saturday, May 23

The Falcon and Vista Ridge ceremonies are open to all — no tickets required, first-come first-served seating. Come early to ensure a good spot for such a big day.

Broadmoor World Arena is off Venetucci Boulevard on the southwest side of Colorado Springs. Parking is available on site.

Earlier this month, Patriot High School and PPEC/SSAE graduates celebrated their ceremonies at COS City Hub on May 15. Now it's the big three's turn.

Congratulations to all D49 seniors and their families — you've earned this.

What's your graduation weekend tradition in your household? Share below.

stories.title: CPSC Recall — Vevor Baby Swings (Suffocation Hazard, ~1,020 Units)

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
CPSC recall: ~1,020 Vevor baby swings. Incline exceeds 10 degrees — violates the Safe Sleep for Babies Act. Suffocation risk.

Models BB501K, BB702A, BB005K. Sold Amazon + Vevor.com. Stop use.

cpsc.gov/Recalls

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
If your baby's swing has "VEVOR" on the base, check the CPSC recall issued this year.

~1,020 swings recalled for inclined sleeper ban violation — suffocation risk.

Full refund available. 855-599-6320 or cpsc.gov

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 12:00 PM MT

If you have a Vevor baby swing at home, please read this.

Sanven Technology has issued a recall of approximately 1,020 Vevor baby swings due to a serious suffocation hazard. The swings have an incline angle that exceeds 10 degrees — violating the mandatory safety standard for infant sleep products and the ban on inclined sleepers under the Safe Sleep for Babies Act.

The affected swings come in three models: white/gray with a white base (BB501K), white/gray with a gray base (BB702A), and black/gray with a black base (BB005K). All have the "VEVOR" trademark on the base. They were sold on Vevor.com and Amazon.com from January 2025 through August 2025, priced between $65 and $80.

Stop using the swing immediately. To receive a full refund, you'll need to cut the swing cover in half, write "Recalled" and the date on the base with a permanent marker, take a photo, and send it to recalling@vevor.com. Then dispose of the swing according to your local waste guidelines.

To start your refund or get more information, call 855-599-6320 Monday through Friday, 9 AM-5 PM PT. The full recall notice is on CPSC.gov.

This is the third recall alert we've shared this week. It's a good time to check your home for any recalled products — CPSC.gov keeps a complete list.

Do you have a Vevor swing at home? Let us know in the comments if this helped.

---

article.headline: CPSC Recall: Vevor Baby Swings Recalled for Suffocation Risk — Stop Use Immediately

stories.title: CPSC Recall — Bellabu Bear Children's Sherpa Robes (Burn Hazard, ~1,300 Units)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Recall: ~1,300 Bellabu Bear sherpa robes. Fail flammability standards — burn risk.

Prints: PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Minions, Batman, Wonder Woman. Sold at Saks + boutiques, ~$60.

Refund: cpsc.gov

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 5:00 PM MT

One more safety alert for parents today.

Bellabu Bear has recalled approximately 1,300 bamboo sherpa children's robes after testing showed they fail the mandatory federal flammability standard for children's sleepwear — posing a burn risk. No injuries have been reported, but the robes shouldn't be used.

These are bamboo sherpa robes in popular licensed prints you might recognize: PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Minions, Batman, Justice League, Wonder Woman, Blaze and the Monster Machines, Trolls, Dora the Explorer, and others, as well as seasonal designs including Nutcracker, hearts, Santa, and stripes. They come in sizes S/M and M/L. The label says "Bellabu Bear," "BB-NW12109," and the size.

The robes were sold at Saks Fifth Avenue, boutiques, and on Bellabubear.com from January 2024 through July 2025 for about $60.

To get a full refund or store credit, stop using the robe and contact Bellabu Bear at 888-703-7752 (Monday-Friday, 9 AM-4 PM PT) or email help@bellabubear.com. The full recall notice is at CPSC.gov.

If you gave these as a gift or received one as a shower present, it's worth a quick check.

Do you have one of these robes in your home? Share this post so other parents can check.

---

article.headline: CPSC Recall: Bellabu Bear Children's Sherpa Robes Recalled for Burn Hazard

stories.title: COS Memorial Day Weekend Family Guide — Territory Days, D49 Grad, Splash Pads Countdown

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
COS families, here's Memorial Day weekend:

Territory Days: Sat-Mon, Old Colorado City (free)
D49 graduation: Saturday, Broadmoor World Arena
Splash pads open: Tuesday May 26, free

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 3:00 PM MT

Memorial Day weekend is officially here, Colorado Springs families — and you've got options.

Here's what's happening this weekend and into next week:

TERRITORY DAYS (May 23-25): The 50th anniversary of Territory Days kicks off Saturday on Colorado Avenue in Old Colorado City. This free three-day festival has over 180 food and craft booths, live music, a kids' zone, petting zoo, gold panning, Wild West gunfight re-enactments, and more. Hours: Saturday-Sunday 10 AM-7 PM, Monday 10 AM-6 PM. Parking tip: use the C.A.B. Garage downtown ($1/hr) with a free shuttle to the festival.

D49 GRADUATION (Saturday): Three District 49 high schools — Falcon, Vista Ridge, and Sand Creek — hold commencement ceremonies at Broadmoor World Arena on Saturday. No tickets required. See our earlier post for times.

SPLASH PADS OPENING (Tuesday, May 26): Panorama Park Splash Pad and Venezia Park Watering Hole both open Tuesday, May 26 at 10 AM. Both are free and run through summer. Start planning your first splash pad day of the year.

PPLD SUMMER ADVENTURE: Registration is open now at ppld.org/summer for the "Unearth a Story" summer reading program. It kicks off June 2 — the same week as D11's last day of school. Good timing to get signed up.

It's one of the busiest, best weekends of the year in COS. Enjoy every bit of it.

What's on your family's agenda this weekend? Share in the comments.

---

article.headline: Colorado Springs Memorial Day Weekend Family Guide 2026: Territory Days, Graduation, and Splash Pads
